Here is the information about Pedilanthus tithymaloides: 

Pedilanthus tithymaloides, commonly known as the crown of thorns or Devil's backbone, is a succulent plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pedilanthus tithymaloides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pedilanthus tithymaloides include teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other health issues.
taxonomy: Pedilonum scabrifolium is classified under the family Rubiaceae. This classification places it within the order Gentianales, which includes other flowering plant families such as Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Members of this family are often found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Pedilonum scabrifolium is native to New Guinea and is a part of this broader family.
